question: Does the model correctly conclude that line BC is tangent to circle O in Part (1)?
Does the model provide the correct distance from center O to line segment AD for Part (2) (Answer: 2√13/3)?
Does the model provide the correct value for the ratio BE/BD in Part (3) (Answer: 2/3)?
In Part (1), does the model correctly identify that OA = OD (radii) to establish that triangle OAD is isosceles (or ∠OAD = ∠ODA)?
In Part (1), does the model correctly use the angle bisector property and the isosceles triangle property to prove OD || AC (or ∠ODA = ∠DAC)?
In Part (1), does the model correctly conclude that OD ⊥ BC using the relationship AC ⊥ BC and OD || AC (or equivalent angle calculation)?
In Part (2), is the length of AD correctly calculated as 2√13 (or √52) using the Pythagorean theorem?
In Part (2), does the model correctly identify that the perpendicular from center O to chord AD bisects the chord (implying AF = √13)?
In Part (2), does the model correctly use similarity (e.g., △AOF ~ △ADC) or trigonometric ratios to find the distance OF?
In Part (3), does the model correctly identify that ∠ADE = 90° because AE is the diameter of the circle?
In Part (3), does the model correctly use tan(∠DAC) = 2/3 to determine the ratio of sides in △ADE (specifically ED/AD)?
In Part (3), does the model correctly establish similarity between △BED and △BDA (or use equivalent geometric logic) to derive the ratio BE/BD?
Are all arithmetic calculations and algebraic manipulations in the solution correct?
Does the generated image clearly depict the basic geometric setup: Right Triangle ABC, Angle Bisector AD, and Circle O with diameter AE?
Are the auxiliary lines mentioned in the reasoning (e.g., OD, OF, ED) drawn as straight, distinct lines in the image?
Does the image correctly show point D lying on the intersection of line segment BC and Circle O?
Does the image correctly show points A and E on the circle, with O visually positioned as the center of diameter AE?
Is the angle at vertex C visually depicted as a right angle (90°) in the generated image?
If the perpendicular OF is drawn, does it visually meet AD at a right angle?
Do point labels (A, B, C, D, E, O) remain consistent in position and identity across all generated images?
If the text mentions connecting OD, is the line segment OD present in the corresponding generated image?
If the text mentions drawing a perpendicular OF to AD, is this segment present in the image?
If the text mentions connecting ED, is the line segment ED present in the image?
Does the visual orientation of OD relative to BC in the image support the text's conclusion that they are perpendicular?
Does the visual representation of AD in the image support the text description of it being the angle bisector of ∠BAC?
If the text states that ∠ADE is 90°, does the image visually depict a right angle at D?
